notes:
For each game proton create prefix in "compatdata/STEAMID". 
Tweaks for Gothic:
1) env WINEPREFIX="YOUR_STEAM_LIBRARY/steamapps/compatdata/39510/pfx/" winetricks directmusic dsound
2) winecfg for prefix -> Libraries. Or edit user.reg in "pfx" folder. 
Look for the entry for dsound and delete it. (This avoids problems with "C: failed to initialize sound, MSS reported: Broken waveOut driver – ’buffer done’ flag never set" errors.)

Perfomance with SystemPack 1.7:
Khorinis (Range of view - 200%): 40-50 fps,
Khorinis (Range of view - 160%): 60 fps,
"Forest" (Range of view  - any%): 60 fps.
title: Gothic II: Gold Edition

notes:
First I updated my nvidia graphics driver to 396 and then I followed this guide https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1496419226 to setup WINEPREFIX and WINEPATH and to install winetricks. It could be necessary to replace your different Proton version in the path. Then you run the following command on your terminal: winetricks directmusic dsound. After that you run winecfg and go to libraries and delete the dsound overwriting. The Tweaks aren't new as three or more people before reported these but because I hadn't set up the wine variables it took me a long time to figure this out. Also I didn't know winetricks and winecfg as tools. I just played a few minutes but it seems very stable. In case the website is not reachable anymore I post the guide below.
WineCFG: Chances are, you are going to run games that are not supported by Proton, and you might have to go into winecfg to get the fix you need. Step 1 Change your wine prefix to the proton prefix: env WINEPREFIX="/YOUR_PATH_TO_LIBRARY/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton 3.16-6 Beta/dist/share/default_pfx". Then, change your wine path to the wine path in the Proton directory WINEPATH="/YOUR_PATH_TO_LIBRARY/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton 3.16-6 Beta/dist/bin/wine64". Step 2 WineCFG: Just type in winecfg into the terminal. Winetricks: There is probably some DLLs that you will need to install to run your unsupported games. Step 1: Do the entirety of Step 1 from the WineCFG section above. Step 2: You will need to grab the Winetricks script: Debian/Ubuntu/Linux Mint: sudo apt-get install winetricks   Arch Linux: yaourt winetricks  Then follow the instructions that yaourt gives. Red Hat/CentOS/Fedora: yum install winetricks   openSUSE: zypper install winetricks   Then after that, you can run winetricks, and it will install to the Proton wine directory.
title: Gothic II: Gold Edition

notes:
I you have a problem with constant resolution change go to the Gothic.ini and
change zVidResFullscreenBPP=32. Above you can change the resolution if you want to. Also set extendedMenu=1 to set the changes permanently.
First I updated my nvidia graphics driver to 396 and then I followed this guide
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1496419226
to setup WINEPREFIX and WINEPATH and to install winetricks. If you follow the guide it is necessary to replace ~/ this with your actual path to the library. It could also be necessary to replace your different Proton version in the path. Then you run the following command on your terminal: winetricks directmusic dsound. After that you run winecfg and go to libraries and delete the dsound overwriting.

I just played a few minutes but it seems very stable.
In case the website is not reachable anymore I post the guide below:

WineCFG:
Chances are, you are going to run games that are not supported by Proton, and you might have to go into winecfg to get the fix you need.

Step 1:
Change your wine prefix to the proton prefix:
env WINEPREFIX="/YOUR_PATH_TO_LIBRARY/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton 3.16-6 Beta/dist/share/default_pfx".
Then, change your wine path to the wine path in the Proton directory
WINEPATH="/YOUR_PATH_TO_LIBRARY/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton 3.16-6 Beta/dist/bin/wine64".
Step 2:

WineCFG:

Just type in winecfg into the terminal

Winetricks:
There is probably some DLLs that you will need to install to run your unsupported games.

Step 1:

Do the entirety of Step 1 from the WineCFG section above.

Step 2:

You will need to grab the Winetricks script:

Debian/Ubuntu/Linux Mint:
sudo apt-get install winetricks.

Arch Linux:
yaourt winetricks.
Then follow the instructions that yaourt gives.

Red Hat/CentOS/Fedora:
yum install winetricks.

openSUSE:
zypper install winetricks.

Then after that, you can run winetricks, and it will install to the Proton wine directory.

That's it, game works perfectly

title: Gothic II: Gold Edition

notes: Since many people have trouble running the game. I've decided to put detailed solution to run the game at good performance. You need wine and winetricks installed on your machine in addition to Steam. 1. Install the game and setup desired language in Steam(Steam->Library->Gothic->Properties->Language). 2.Run the game -> it crashes. 3.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/39510/pfx/ winetricks directmusic" 4.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/39510/pfx/ winecfg" 5.Go to Libraries tab, in existing overrides search for 'dsound', select it and press remove button. 6.Run the game -> it runs, but probably it won't load at optimal screen resolution. 7.In gothic.ini: "~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic II/system/Gothic.ini" search in INTERNAL section for extendedMenu=0 and change the value to 1 and save file. Now when you run the game you can chose more screen resolutions  8.Have fun! 9.Don't bork the game without trying this.
title: Gothic II: Gold Edition

cpu: AMD A8-3870 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Game did not start up at all. Following the steps indicated here, the game did start but the screen / window would flicker and keep resizing itself while remaining black at all times. Trying to change Proton version or the OS (to Windows XP) did not fix it. Finally, I went to the .ini config file and turned on the option zStartupWindowed=1. This enables windowed mode. I set the resolution to 1024 * 768 which seems to work fine, as well as 800 * 600. Of course it is not the perfect solution after all but at least the game works fine. Performance is also good!
title: Gothic II: Gold Edition

notes: I finished the entire game without any disturbing problems. Sometimes the sound didn't play correctly, but reloading the save did the trick. To get it running, I followed the guide posted in a former report: "1. Install the game and setup desired language in Steam(Steam->Library->Gothic->Properties->Language). 2.Run the game -> it crashes. 3.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/39510/pfx/ winetricks directmusic" 4.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/39510/pfx/ winecfg" 5.Go to Libraries tab, in existing overrides search for 'dsound', select it and press remove button. 6.Run the game -> it runs, but probably it won't load at optimal screen resolution. 7.In gothic.ini: "~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic II/system/Gothic.ini" search in INTERNAL section for extendedMenu=0 and change the value to 1 and save file. Now when you run the game you can chose more screen resolutions" In addition to get the cutscenes working (instead of a black screen with only sound), I had to set scaleVideos to 1(!) in Gothic.ini, as it was set to 0 by default.
title: Gothic II: Gold Edition

notes: Game doesn't work out of the box on Proton, like mentioned by others I had to install directmusic and set in winecfg dsound to Wine to get the game working. I copied manually dgVoodoo2, run protontricks GAMEID winecfg, set ddraw to native and then the game worked on Vulkan with DXVK even when testing with the new ACO compiler.
title: Gothic II: Gold Edition

title: Gothic II: Gold Edition

notes:
Solid 60 FPS at 1440p on my system. No issues with sound. No external patches/fixes were needed. It was mentioned before, but for the sake of having complete information here is what worked:
1. Install the game and setup desired language in Steam(Steam->Library->Gothic->Properties->Language). 
2.Run the game -> it crashes. 
3.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/39510/pfx/ winetricks directmusic" 
4.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/39510/pfx/ winecfg" 5.Go to Libraries tab, in existing overrides search for 'dsound', select it and press remove button. 
6.Run the game -> it runs, but probably it won't load at optimal screen resolution. 7.In gothic.ini: "~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic II/system/Gothic.ini" search in INTERNAL section for extendedMenu=0 and change the value to 1 and save file. Now when you run the game you can chose more screen resolutions" In addition to get the cutscenes working (instead of a black screen with only sound), I had to set scaleVideos to 1(!) in Gothic.ini, as it was set to 0 by default.
title: Gothic II: Gold Edition
